p.em: This diagram details the logic for resolving field addresses within a flat-buffer structure.

div.p: asm ; rdi = table_start_ ; esi = field_id movsxd rax, dword ptr [rdi] ; 1. Load soffset (signed extend) lea rcx, [rdi + rax] ; 2. Compute vtable addr movzx eax, word ptr [rcx + rsi*2] ; 3. Load uint16 field offset test ax, ax ; 4. Check if offset is zero je .return_default add rdi, rax ; 5. Final field_ptr mov eax, [rdi] ; 6. Actual value load ret .return_default: mov eax, [rdx] ; Load fallback default ret

ul.li: VTable: Pre-computed metadata stored before the data. Contains field offsets.
soffset: 4-byte signed integer at table_start_. Points back to VTable.
Alignment: Fields are typically aligned to their natural width (2/4/8 bytes).
